company_model.business_archetype: Fabless IC design: designs NOR/NAND/DRAM memory, MCU, sensors, analog chips; outsources manufacturing; sells to ODM/OEM and distributors globally.
company_model.value_proposition_and_customers: Provides critical semiconductor components for industrial, automotive, consumer, AI/cloud, IoT, and networking. Customers include module makers, device OEMs, ODMs, and distributors with growing China localization demand.
company_model.revenue_equation: Revenue = Σ(segment volume × ASP). Volume depends on design wins, foundry wafer allocation, and end-demand. ASP driven by memory cycle (NOR, SLC NAND, DRAM), product mix shift to higher-value SKUs, and ASP for MCU/analog.
company_model.profit_equation: Gross profit = revenue – wafer costs – packaging/test costs – other COGS. Operating profit = gross profit – R&D (~17% of revenue) – SG&A. Non-operating items may include fair value changes on investments; need to isolate recurring net profit.
company_model.cash_flow_equation: OCF = net profit + D&A – working capital changes (inventory, receivables). Fabless model requires low capex; major reinvestment is R&D expense (already in P&L). OCF/net profit ratio historically >1x, indicating high cash conversion.
company_model.capital_intensity_and_reinvestment: Capital-light: fabless design house with moderate R&D expense. Revenue growth requires working capital build (inventory), but asset base is mainly cash, financial investments, and some tangible assets for test/design. Reinvestment is R&D for next-gen products and customer customization.
company_model.share_count_period: 2026-07-20; latest stock_basic; Tushare stock_basic.reg_capital (CNY 10,000) / 100; CNY 1 par value
moat_mechanisms: IP and design know-how in NOR flash, MCU, and sensors accumulated over years.
Broad product portfolio (memory+MCU+sensors+analog) enabling integrated platform solutions and cross-selling.
Customer stickiness due to qualification cycles, software ecosystem (MCU SDK), and customized chip designs.
Scale in NOR flash (top-tier global supplier) providing cost advantages and foundry relationship leverage.
moat_to_financial_transmission: IP and design wins → premium ASP and higher gross margins vs generic commodity.
Platform approach → lower customer churn and more predictable revenue.
Qualification/design-in → switching cost for industrial/auto customers, supporting revenue resilience.
Scale in NOR → better wafer pricing and cost absorption, sustaining margins in down cycles.
structural_risks: Memory price cyclicality: NOR/NAND/DRAM ASP can decline sharply if supply exceeds demand.
Dependence on external foundry capacity (TSMC, SMIC, etc.); supply constraints could limit volume.
Intense competition from Winbond, Macronix, Puya, and IDM incumbents; Chinese peers ramping similar products.
Trade restrictions and geopolitical risk could disrupt wafer supply or export markets.
Technology obsolescence: new memory technologies (e.g., MRAM, ReRAM) or advanced logic embedded NVM could displace NOR.
Equity incentive dilution and potential share count overhang.
key_unknowns: Product-level revenue and margin for memory, MCU, sensors, analog in FY2025 and 2026E.
Exact non-recurring composition of H1 2026 net profit and sustainable recurring profit run-rate.
Diluted share count including stock options, restricted shares, and potential convertible instruments.
Foundry wafer allocation and capacity outlook for 2026-2028.
ASP trajectory for specific products (NOR, SLC NAND, DRAM) and duration of upcycle.
Capex and R&D capitalisation policy; free cash flow generation.
ChangXin Technology IPO stake value and ultimate financial treatment (FVOCI, no income impact, but asset value).

business_unit_map.economic_role: Largest revenue and profit pool; products: NOR flash, SLC NAND, DRAM; driven by memory cycle, AI and industrial demand.
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Memory revenue = Σ(memory product volume × ASP); volume from design wins, foundry allocation; ASP from memory cycle and mix.
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Memory gross margin = (ASP – unit cost)/ASP, with unit cost including wafer, packaging, test; operating margin after allocated R&D/SG&A.
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: Inventory building in upcycle; OCF strong due to high margins and cash conversion >1x; working capital needs increase with revenue growth.
missing_inputs: FY2025 segment revenue and gross margin for memory products from annual report
NOR vs NAND vs DRAM mix and volume/ASP data
Foundry wafer cost and packaging/test cost breakdown
business_unit_map.next_verification: Retrieve 2025 annual report product segment table (EV031) and interim Q2 2026 report.

business_unit_map.economic_role: Second profit engine; MCU products for industrial, automotive, IoT; benefiting from domestic substitution and AI edge integration.
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: MCU revenue = MCU shipment × ASP; shipment driven by design wins and embedded software ecosystem.
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: MCU gross margin historically lower than memory but improving with higher-end SKUs; R&D intensity high.
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: Inventory moderate; relatively fast cash conversion.
missing_inputs: MCU segment revenue, ASP trend, gross margin FY2025
MCU product mix (32-bit vs 8-bit, AI MCU)
business_unit_map.next_verification: Retrieve FY2025 segment reporting and MCU market data.

business_unit_map.economic_role: Smaller segment: fingerprint sensors, touch controllers; linked to smartphone/consumer cycles.
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Sensor revenue = unit shipments × ASP; driven by smartphone adoption and new designs.
missing_inputs: Sensor segment revenue and margin FY2025

business_unit_map.economic_role: New growth vector: power management, signal chain; early ramp, small revenue base.
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Analog revenue = units × ASP; ramp depends on design wins and market penetration.
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Initially low margin due to high R&D and low volume; scaling could bring leverage.
missing_inputs: Analog revenue and product categories FY2025
Design win pipeline and target markets
business_unit_map.next_verification: FY2025 annual report or IR update.

segment_models.customers_and_channel: Distributors, industrial/automotive OEMs, AI server builders, consumer electronics brands; global with increasing China localization
demand_and_order_drivers: AI server and edge AI require NOR and SLC NAND for boot code and configuration storage
Industrial IoT and automotive electronics ramp; increasing content per device
Consumer wearables, TWS, smartphones feature higher memory density
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Global NOR supply dominated by Winbond, Macronix, GigaDevice; older nodes tight as foundries focus on logic; SLC NAND supply further constrained as mainstream NAND makers shift to MLC/TLC; DRAM supply oligopolistic, AI crowding out legacy DRAM.
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: GigaDevice gaining market share as competitors face capacity limits; design wins translating into high utilization of allotted foundry capacity.
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: ASP surging: NOR up 20-40% YoY; SLC NAND projected +120-170% in H2 2026 per TrendForce; DRAM prices firm.
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Wafer prices rising but ASP jumps outpace cost inflation; packaging and test costs stable.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Gross margin expanded from ~38% in FY2025 to 57% in Q1 2026; could exceed 60% in H2 2026; operating margin above 40% due to operating leverage.
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: Inventory days falling (inventory/revenue ratio -11.89pp YoY); OCF/net profit ratio 1.22x, sign of strong cash conversion even in growth mode.
segment_models.causal_analysis: AI-driven demand + constrained supply → sharp ASP increase → gross margin expansion → strong operating leverage → high net income and cash flow. Cycle sustainability depends on duration of supply tightness.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Core earnings; likely valued on forward P/E; multiple will compress when cycle peaks.
strongest_counterevidence: Memory cycles historically mean-revert rapidly; current high margins may attract new capacity and price competition.
Market-implied PE TTM of 105x suggests the market already prices a massive recovery and is sceptical about duration.
missing_inputs: FY2025 memory segment revenue, gross margin
Product-level ASP data (NOR, SLC NAND, DRAM)
segment_models.next_verification: Retrieve FY2025 annual report product table; track Q2 2026 interim financials and ASP data.

segment_models.customers_and_channel: Industrial control, automotive body electronics, IoT devices, smart home; mainly domestic OEMs and distributors
demand_and_order_drivers: Industrial automation recovery driving MCU demand
Automotive electronics content increase (body control, sensors)
China domestic substitution for overseas MCU brands
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Global MCU market large; major players STM, NXP, Microchip; domestic players gaining share; foundry capacity for mature nodes (40nm-130nm) ample.
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: Company gaining design wins in industrial and auto, but volume smaller vs memory; utilization ramping.
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: ASP improving as mix shifts to higher-end 32-bit and AI-enabled MCUs.
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Wafer costs on mature nodes competitive; R&D expense high as % of revenue.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Gross margin mid-40% range, operating margin mid-teens; improvement through scale and product mix.
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: Inventory levels moderate; OCF generally positive.
segment_models.causal_analysis: Industrial recovery and localization tailwinds drive volume; mix shift raises ASP and margin; but segment smaller than memory.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Second core engine; valued together with memory or on separate MCU peer multiples.
strongest_counterevidence: Competition from domestic MCU peers could limit ASP upside.
Industrial recovery pace uncertain; inventory still elevated at some customers.
missing_inputs: MCU ASP and unit volume data
segment_models.next_verification: FY2025 annual report; Q2 2026 management commentary.

segment_models.customers_and_channel: Smartphone OEMs, wearable device makers; mostly domestic
demand_and_order_drivers: Adoption of AMOLED displays requiring advanced touch controllers
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Mature market; competition from dedicated sensor firms; fabless model with standard CMOS nodes, no supply constraint.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Gross margin 30-40%; contributes low absolute profit.
segment_models.causal_analysis: Tied to smartphone cycle; no major growth dynamic.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Minor contributor; included in consolidated earnings.
strongest_counterevidence: Smartphone saturation; competition may reduce margins.

segment_models.products_or_services: Power management ICs, signal chain analog
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Industrial, consumer, automotive; early customers, likely distributors
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Large global market; highly fragmented; no supply constraint.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Very low or negative margin due to initial R&D.
segment_models.causal_analysis: Potential future growth but currently immaterial.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Optionality; not included in core value unless revenues scale with proven margin.
strongest_counterevidence: Very small scale; may never achieve profitability.
missing_inputs: Analog revenue, design wins, margin plan
segment_models.next_verification: FY2025 annual report and pipeline disclosures.

underwriting_questions.question: What is the exact split of H1 2026 parent net profit of 6900 million CNY between recurring core earnings and non-recurring items (e.g., investment gains, asset sales, fair value changes)?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Directly determines the sustainable earning power used for forward P/E valuation. If a large portion is one-off, the quality of earnings is lower and the market may re-rate the stock downward once the composition is disclosed.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Unresolved: sell-side estimates suggest recurring net profit ~3500 mn vs total 6900 mn, implying 3400 mn non-recurring; company claims no material undisclosed non-recurring items, but the conflict indicates note-level disclosure required.
bull_evidence: Company statements via investor interaction say ChangXin IPO fair value changes have no direct impact on net profit (FVOCI classification). (EV investor_interaction)
Management reiterated in risk-reminder announcement that guidance is based on actual operating data; no undisclosed large one-offs implied.
bear_evidence: DW electronics channel check estimates Q2 net profit 5400 mn but recurring only 3500 mn, a ~1900 mn delta; that suggests large non-operating gains. (KPE05)
Past financial reports have included fair value gains from investments; a large mark-to-market on other equity holdings could inflate net profit.
missing_evidence: Detailed income statement for H1 2026, including non-recurring items and segment-level operating vs non-operating classification
underwriting_questions.next_verification: 2026 semi-annual report (expected Aug 2026) and management's explanation of non-recurring items.

underwriting_questions.question: What are the realized and expected ASP trends for NOR flash, SLC NAND, and specialty DRAM in 2026-2028, and what is the impact on revenue and gross margin trajectory?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Memory ASP is the primary driver of revenue growth and gross margin expansion. If the cycle peaks in 2H 2026 and then normalizes, the current high earnings may not be sustainable.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Unverified proxies: TrendForce forecasts SLC NAND price +120-170% in H2 2026; multiple KPEs mention tight supply and price hikes, but no company-specific realized ASP data. Q1 gross margin 57% suggests significant ASP increase already captured.
bull_evidence: KeyBanc raised DRAM/NAND price forecasts after Asia supply chain survey; supply tight through 2027. (KPE01)
TrendForce estimates SLC NAND 120-170% increase in H2 2026. (KPE03)
Q1 2026 gross margin expansion of 19.6pp YoY implies strong ASP increases already feeding through. (EV013)
bear_evidence: Sell-side report mentions 'MLC > SLC > niche DDR > NOR' in price strength order, suggesting NOR could be last to benefit and less dramatic. (KPE05)
Historical memory cycles have seen rapid price normalization when new capacity comes online.
missing_evidence: Company-specific realized ASP per product category (NOR, NAND, DRAM) quarterly
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Quarterly data from supply chain trackers (TrendForce, IC Insights) and Q2 2026 semi-annual report segment disclosure.

underwriting_questions.question: What is the actual allocated foundry wafer capacity for GigaDevice in 2026-2028 across TSMC, SMIC, and other foundries, and can it support the volume growth needed to meet the strong demand?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Without sufficient capacity, even strong orders cannot translate into volume and revenue. The fabless model's volume ceiling is determined by foundry allocation.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: No disclosed capacity figures. Analysts assume company can secure incremental capacity from foundries, but this is unverified.
bull_evidence: Company management claims to maintain strong relationships with foundry partners; has historically increased supply. (EV030)
Sell-side notes that overseas competitors' capacity is tight, potentially shifting demand to GigaDevice. (KPE06)
bear_evidence: Foundry capacity on mature nodes is limited; many chip designers compete for the same lines; allocation may be insufficient to meet surging demand.
Geopolitical issues could restrict access to certain foundries (e.g., TSMC limits).
missing_evidence: Foundry wafer allocation agreements, capacity outlook statements from company or foundry partners
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Management commentary on capacity outlook in 2026H1 earnings call; foundry public capex plans.

underwriting_questions.question: What is the diluted share count as of July 2026, including outstanding stock options, restricted shares, and potential convertible bonds?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: EPS, per-share valuation, and fair value calculations require an accurate diluted share count. A large option overhang could significantly reduce EPS and fair value per share.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Unresolved. No reliable share count available. Q1 seasonal share of 26.0% is a profit seasonality metric, not share count. Market cap of 303.36 bn divided by an unknown stock price yields no solution.
bull_evidence: Company has a history of share buybacks and limited dilution; past convertible bond plans may be low. (EV026 mentions stock option cancellation, not issuance).
Possible stock option cancellations (EV027) could reduce potential overhang.
bear_evidence: Equity incentive plans are common in tech firms; many options outstanding from previous grants could increase diluted share count.
If the share price is high, more options are in-the-money and dilutive.
missing_evidence: Most recent total shares from Tushare stock_basic or daily_basic
Company filing of share capital (annual report, quarterly)
Stock option plan details and dilution calculation
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Retrieve total share capital from Tushare / CNINFO filings; check for ESOP and option grants in 2025 annual report.

underwriting_questions.question: What is the capex and R&D spending plan for 2026-2028, and what free cash flow can be generated after sustaining investment?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Even if net profit is high, heavy capex or R&D capitalisation could consume cash and reduce FCF, affecting dividend capacity and intrinsic value.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: No capex breakdown given. Fabless model implies low tangible capex; R&D is expensed, not capitalized, so FCF should be high in the upcycle. Historical R&D ~17% of revenue. But confirmation needed.
bull_evidence: Historical OCF/net profit ratio of 1.22x in Q1 2026 indicates strong cash conversion; low capex typical for fabless. (EV017)
bear_evidence: Rapid revenue growth may require working capital investment (inventory, receivables) reducing FCF.
If company invests in testing or R&D facilities, capex could rise.
missing_evidence: FY2025 and Q1 2026 cash flow statements (specifically capex line)
Management guidance on capex and R&D capital allocation
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Retrieve cash flow statement from Tushare or financial reports; review 2025 annual report management discussion.

underwriting_questions.question: How sustainable is the current gross margin above 55% beyond 2026, especially if the memory cycle turns or competitors add capacity?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Long-term valuation multiple depends on normalized margins, not peak-cycle figures. If the market already prices in peak margins, any sign of normalization could trigger de-rating.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Unclear. Gross margin 57% in Q1 2026 vs 37% in FY2025; some of this is cyclical ASP spike, but product mix shift and higher NOR/DRAM value-add may create a higher floor. However, history shows that margins revert when supply catches up.
bull_evidence: Company's increasing focus on high-density NOR, automotive-grade, and customized memory with longer design-in cycles could sustain a structural margin uplift.
AI edge needs for high-reliability NOR may create a sticky premium tier.
bear_evidence: Memory industry has a history of boom-bust cycles; gross margins of 60%+ attract new entry and capacity, eventually leading to price wars.
Market-implied PE 105x on TTM suggests expectation of rapid normalization, not sustained high margins.
missing_evidence: Industry capacity addition plans from competitors (Winbond, Macronix, Puya) and foundry capex
Company's product mix breakdown and high-margin product share targets
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Monitor industry supply announcements; review management's long-term margin guidance during H1 2026 briefing.

forecast_lines.formula: 2026E: H1 guidance 11,500 mn + estimated H2 13,500 mn (price/volume growth continues); 2027E: +20% Y/Y; 2028E: +10% Y/Y
Assume peak gross margin in 2026E (Q1 57.08% trend), gradually normalizing as supply improves
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Memory ASP normalization and wafer cost

forecast_lines.formula: Operating margin = gross margin - R&D (17% of revenue) - SG&A (3% of revenue) with operating leverage assumption
Based on H1 guidance 6900 mn and assumed H2 contribution; includes possible non-recurring items, so note quality
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Non-recurring items classification and memory cycle duration

forecast_lines.formula: Parent net profit / diluted share count; share count missing
missing_inputs: Diluted share count (total shares + options + convertible)

forecast_lines.formula: Net profit × cash conversion ratio of ~1.2 (based on Q1 2026 ratio); assumes quality maintained

missing_inputs: Cash paid for acquisition of fixed assets and intangibles

forecast_lines.formula: OCF - Capex; both inputs have missing components
parent net profit (CNY mn) / diluted shares (mn)

operating_assumptions: Memory ASP continues to rise through 2028 due to deep supply crunch, with NOR and SLC NAND prices 2-3x above 2025 levels.
GigaDevice secures substantial additional foundry capacity, allowing 25% volume growth in 2027 and 2028.
Non-recurring items are minimal; reported parent net profit is fully operational.
Market assigns premium P/E multiple of 25x on 2026E net profit of 20 bn CNY, reflecting growth and quality.
scenarios.valuation_method: P/E multiple 25x on 2026E net profit; need diluted shares to determine per-share value
scenarios.falsification: If industry ASP data shows price rollover in Q1 2027, or foundry capacity growth disappoints.

operating_assumptions: Memory prices remain elevated in 2026, moderate increase in 2027, and begin normalizing in 2028.
Volume growth modest at 10-15% as capacity allocation is adequate but not ample.

Parent net profit H1 2026 6900 mn includes ~3400 mn non-recurring items, so sustainable annual recurring profit around 12,000 mn (implied H1 recurring ~3500 mn, H2 similar, total 12 bn). Allowing for some growth, base case total 15 bn.
Market applies 20x P/E on 2026 net profit, roughly at current levels.
scenarios.falsification: If semi-annual report reveals recurring net profit substantially lower (<3000 mn in H1), base case profit would be revised down.

operating_assumptions: Memory cycle peaks in Q2 2026 and prices decline rapidly in 2027-2028.
Foundry allocation grows but demand weakens, leading to inventory build and eventual ASP erosion.
Parent net profit H1 2026 includes large one-off; sustainable annual net profit only 8-10 bn.
Market contracts P/E multiple to 15x on deteriorating outlook.
scenarios.falsification: If SLC NAND or NOR spot prices decline >10% QoQ in Q4 2026.

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: Memory cycle intensity and duration determine revenue and gross margin
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Revenue = memory volume × ASP; gross margin = (ASP - unit cost)/ASP
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: ASP stays elevated through 2027, then normalizes by 2028
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: ASP continues to rise to 2028, supply remains tight
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: ASP peaks in Q2 2026 and declines rapidly in 2027
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Could swing fair value per share by ±50% due to multiplier effect on earnings and multiple
missing_inputs: Segment revenue base, ASP trajectory, unit cost

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: Sustainability and quality of earnings: recurring vs non-recurring
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Adjusted net profit = reported parent net profit - non-recurring gains/losses
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: ~20% of H1 2026 net profit is non-recurring, leaving 5520 mn recurring for H1
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: Non-recurring items are trivial and reported profit is all operational
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: Non-recurring items are >40% of H1 profit, recurring profit is substantially lower
thesis_financial_bridges.profit_impact: could change EPS run-rate by up to 50%
thesis_financial_bridges.fcf_or_capital_impact: non-recurring items may not generate cash
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Market may assign lower multiple to non-recurring earnings; could reduce fair value per share materially.

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: Diluted share count determines EPS and per-share valuation
thesis_financial_bridges.operating_driver: Share dilution from ESOP and potential convertibles
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Diluted EPS = parent net profit / weighted diluted shares
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: Diluted shares around 850-900 mn (rough estimate based on recent market cap/price? but unknown)
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: Share count stays low (~800 mn) due to buybacks and option cancellations
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: Dilution pushes share count above 1000 mn
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Directly scales per-share value; critical for fair value estimate.
missing_inputs: Total shares outstanding and dilution schedule

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: MCU and analog business recovery offer second growth engine
thesis_financial_bridges.affected_unit: 微控制器产品 and 模拟芯片
thesis_financial_bridges.operating_driver: MCU volume and mix; analog revenue ramp
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Incremental profit = MCU revenue * contribution margin + analog revenue * contribution margin
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: MCU revenue grows 20% pa, analog reaches break-even by 2028
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: MCU surges 40% pa on industrial and auto wins; analog profitable by 2027
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: MCU growth stalls below 10%, analog remains loss-making
thesis_financial_bridges.revenue_impact: could add 2-4 bn revenue in 2028 in bull case
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Could justify separate MCU/business optionality value bucket if high growth.
missing_inputs: MCU and analog segment revenue and margin data

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: Foundry capacity constraint as a ceiling for volume growth
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Memory volume = wafer allocation × yield × die per wafer
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: Able to secure 15% incremental capacity per year
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: Foundry partners prioritize GigaDevice, yielding 25% capacity growth
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: Capacity growth limited to 5% as foundries allocate to other large customers
thesis_financial_bridges.revenue_impact: could cap revenue growth even if demand is strong
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: A capacity cap would limit volume-driven valuation upside.
missing_inputs: Foundry allocation details and wafer input data

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: IP and design know-how in NOR flash and MCU
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Gross margin premium vs. peer (e.g., Puya, Macronix) on similar products; sustained over multiple cycles.
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: Peers: Winbond, Macronix, Puya; compare reported gross margins and ASP realizations.
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: Partially evident: Q1 2026 gross margin 57% is high, but lacking peer comparison data for same period.
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Higher sustained gross margin → higher and less cyclical earnings → premium P/E.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: In previous downcycle (2023), gross margin fell to ~37%, similar to peers, suggesting any premium is cycle-dependent rather than structural.
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Collect peer gross margin data for 2024-2026 to compare across cycles.

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Customer switching costs due to qualification and ecosystem lock-in (MCU SDK)
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Customer retention rate or average engagement length; high proportion of repeat design wins.
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: Company's own track record and MCU competitor stickiness (ST, NXP, etc.)
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: Limited evidence; no public data on retention. Management commentary mentions platform ecosystem, but no measurable churn data.
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: High retention leads to revenue predictability and fends off price competition, supporting margin.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: In MCU market, Chinese customers often switch to lower-cost domestic suppliers rapidly, undermining ecosystem lock-in.
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Customer survey or disclosed design-win repeat rates from IR.

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Scale in NOR flash providing cost advantage and foundry relationship leverage
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Cost per bit lower than smaller peers; consistent unit cost trend.
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: Competitors Macronix, Puya; compare income statement COGS/shipments.
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Lower cost → higher gross margin at same ASP → better profitability in down cycles.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Puya, a Chinese peer, is rapidly scaling and may match cost structure, eroding scale advantage.

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: P/E on adjusted 2026E net profit (base case recurring)
valuation_buckets.metric_and_period: Adjusted net profit 2026E: 12,000 mn (recurring portion, base case estimate)
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: Excludes non-recurring items and non-operating assets; will be summed with ChangXin stake optionality.

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: 1.8% of ChangXin's expected market cap post-IPO, with haircut for lock-up and FVOCI classification (no income recognition)
valuation_buckets.metric_and_period: ChangXin IPO price reference: 8.66 CNY/share per KPE02, but total valuation unknown. Assuming valuation of ~50 bn CNY, stake ~900 mn CNY, extremely small relative to market cap.
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: This asset is already on the balance sheet as FVOCI, but its movements do not affect net profit; its inclusion is as optionality of value realization upon disposal or dividend. No double-count with earnings core because earnings exclude its fair value changes.
missing_inputs: Company's intention to sell or hold
